commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh The wolf and the lamb shall feed together, and the lion shall eat straw like the ox: and dust shall be the serpent's food. They shall not hurt nor destroy in all my holy mountain, says the LORD. People of the most perverse tempers shall become mild by the influence of the gospel, and shall dwell together in perfect concord. (Calmet) Food, according to the sentence, Genesis iii. (Menochius) The devil's power is abridged, chap. (Calmet) The proudest Gentiles are converted, and adopt the mild manners of Christians, in fasting and mortification. (Worthington).
